cadence: one X and one LinkedIn candidate per active business per day

business.name: Agentic Evidence
business.primary_job: Agentic technical interview evidence layer for Hatchways-style assessments.
business.current_motion: Candidate AI-evidence entry, reviewer handoff, repo-packaged Claude JSONL exports, assignment packet, guided Claude VM behavior, and finish checklist are live; 2026-05-04 commit `4f37230` added `/candidate/:sessionId/task`, `ae-task`, `ae-submit-steps`, Claude guidance injection, and procedural-prompt score filtering.
business.next_move: Add hidden-test grading, optional GitHub App install UX, and reviewer analysis fields over captured sessions.

x.copy:
Agentic Evidence gives technical interview teams a replayable packet instead of a gut read.

The live sample includes a candidate VM flow, GitHub Action evidence, session exports, hidden-test results, and a reviewer report.

https://agentic-evidence.fly.dev/sample-report

linkedin.copy:
Agentic Evidence is aimed at one narrow interview problem: reviewers need to see how a candidate worked with AI, not just the final diff.

The live sample packet includes a candidate VM flow, GitHub Action evidence, session exports, hidden-test results, and a reviewer report.

https://agentic-evidence.fly.dev/sample-report

business.name: Bring Your AI
business.primary_job: Local-first Claude Code to Codex harness migration.
business.current_motion: Product, payments, agent-readable commerce, direct Stripe checkout, hold-slot fallback, and `/pricing` page are live after design pass commit `30508b5`; `foundry-businesses/portable` is only a marketing mirror on 1P.
business.next_move: Continue Claude/Codex-user distribution; SPT/ACP and x402/MPP remain private-preview-gated.

x.copy:
Bring Your AI is for the point where Claude Code stops and Codex needs the same operating context.

It keeps the move local: rules, skills, MCP config, hooks, and validation notes.

https://bringyour.ai/claude-code-to-codex

linkedin.copy:
Bring Your AI is for the unglamorous part of switching from Claude Code to Codex: moving the harness state without turning it into a manual rebuild.

Rules, skills, MCP config, hooks, and validation notes stay local, and non-equivalent pieces get written as target-side notes.

https://bringyour.ai/claude-code-to-codex

business.name: AI Dev Board
business.primary_job: AI/ML jobs board with REST API, MCP server, company claims, and promoted listings.
business.current_motion: API/MCP/indexed-job distribution plus employer conversion flow; `/post` plan-first flow and `/jobs` canonical redirect are live after design pass commit `ddc8b65`; `/tag/agents` visual cleanup is live in commit `4c30bea` with `AI Agent Jobs` title/meta and compact hero/search/filter layout; `foundry-businesses/ai-dev-jobs` is only a marketing/outreach mirror on 1P.
business.next_move: Keep API invariant green; improve SEO indexing and hiring-side conversion surfaces.

x.copy:
AI Dev Board is agent-readable by design: REST API, MCP server, job feeds, company pages, and promoted listings.

The useful claim is not "another job board." It is a job board agents can query.

https://aidevboard.com/api/v1

linkedin.copy:
AI Dev Board is built around an agent-readable hiring surface.

The public product has a REST API, MCP server, job feeds, company pages, and promoted listings. Agents can query the market directly instead of scraping a search page.

https://aidevboard.com/api/v1

business.name: Not Human Search
business.primary_job: Agentic-readiness search engine and scoring system.
business.current_motion: Discovery, monitoring, score-fix monetization, score reasons, and clearer search/monitor forms are live after design pass commit `73dc329`; `foundry-businesses/nothumansearch` is only a marketing/outreach mirror on 1P.
business.next_move: Continue distribution directories and improve indexed agent-first coverage.

x.copy:
Not Human Search answers a simple agent question: can this site be used by software, or is it only written for humans?

Scores cover llms.txt, OpenAPI, MCP, structured APIs, manifests, and schema.

https://nothumansearch.ai/score

linkedin.copy:
Not Human Search is a search engine for agent-ready sites.

The scoring surface checks practical signals: llms.txt, OpenAPI, MCP, structured APIs, manifests, and schema. The point is to make agent readiness inspectable instead of implied by marketing copy.

https://nothumansearch.ai/score
